revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:穂積押山]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Q22129270|qid=Q22129270|1=Hozumi Iwayumi}}}}
{{Infobox noble | name = Hozumi Oshiyama | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (late) | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = Unknown | birth_name = | other_names = | posthumous_name = | deified_as = | dharma_name = | burial_place = | Ranks = Governor of Dari Province / Governor of Lower Dari Province | allegiance = {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} | clan = {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} (臣) | parents = | siblings = | spouse = | children = | notable_activities = }}
'''Hozumi Oshiyama''' (穂積 押山) was a figure active in the early 6th century during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. His kabane (hereditary title) was 臣 (Omi).
In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, he is referred to as "Hozumi no Omi Oshiyama" (穂積臣押山). The annotations in the text also note that in the now-lost ''{{ill|Baekje Records|ja|百済本記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baekje Records|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 百済三書|1=Baekje Annals|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11244191}}'', he was called "Iwai no Oshiyama Kimi" (委意斯移麻岐彌). There is no mention of him in the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}''.
He served during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} (26th emperor) as Governor of Dari Province or Lower Dari Province in {{ill|Mimana|en|Mimana|qid=Q1790223|lt=Mimana|1=Mimana}} and played a significant role in the negotiations concerning the transfer of four counties in Mimana to Baekje.
== Historical Records == According to the ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'', in the 6th year of Emperor Keitai's reign (512 CE?), Hozumi Oshiyama was dispatched to [[:en:Baekje|Baekje]] and presented 40 horses from {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|qid=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province|1=Tsukushi Province}}.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki}} Emperor Keitai, 6th year, 4th month, 6th day</ref>
In the 12th month of the same year, Baekje sent envoys to request the transfer of four counties—Upper Dari, Lower Dari, Sada, and Muro—in Mimana. Oshiyama, as Governor of Dari Province, supported the transfer on the grounds that the counties were closer to Baekje than Japan. His proposal was supported by {{ill|Ōtomo no Kanamura|en|Ōtomo no Kanamura|qid=Q11432358|lt=Otomo no Kanamura|1=Ōtomo no Kanamura}}, and the transfer was approved. However, rumors later arose that both Oshiyama and Kanamura had accepted bribes from Baekje.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki}} Emperor Keitai, 6th year, 12th month</ref>
In the 7th year of Emperor Keitai's reign (513 CE?), Baekje offered a Confucian scholar, {{ill|Dan Yangni|ja|段楊爾|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dan Yangni|1=Duan Yang'er|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q19939662}}, to Japan and requested support in recovering the Kibun region from the neighboring Pyeonghae Kingdom. Oshiyama was dispatched to Japan alongside Commander Jeam Moon-Gwi to assist in the discussions.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki}} Emperor Keitai, 7th year, 6th month</ref>
In the 23rd year of Emperor Keitai's reign (529 CE?), Oshiyama, as Governor of Lower Dari Province, reported that Baekje sought to change its trade route to utilize the Tasatsu port in Gaya instead of the traditional locations. Despite opposition from the Gaya King, Japan granted the request, leading Gaya to ally with {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|qid=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}} and harbor resentment toward Japan.<ref>{{Nihon Shoki}} Emperor Keitai, 23rd year, 3rd month</ref>
== Analysis == The records depict Hozumi Oshiyama as a government official serving the Yamato court. Some scholars suggest that Oshiyama acted as an intermediary for Baekje interests, potentially serving as a Yamato emissary who remained in Baekje after the transfer of the Dari region.<ref>{{Cite book|author=朴天秀|title=加耶と倭 韓半島と日本列島の考古学|publisher=講談社|year=2007|isbn=4062583984|page=112}}</ref>
Historian {{ill|Yi Hongjik|ja|李弘稙|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yi Hongjik|1=Hong-jik Yi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16767798}} posits that Oshiyama was initially a Yamato envoy but eventually served as a Baekje official overseeing the Dari region.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=李在碩|title=六世紀代の倭系百済官僚とその本質|journal=駒澤史学|volume=62|pages=32-58|year=2004}}</ref>
The ''Baekje Records'' referring to Oshiyama as "Iwai no Oshiyama Kimi" (委意斯移麻岐彌) suggests a close relationship between Oshiyama and the Baekje court, similar to other figures granted honorary titles by Baekje kings.<ref>{{Cite journal|author=李在碩|year=2004|pages=36}}</ref>
== References == * 吉村武彦, ed. {{Cite book|title=国史大辞典|publisher=吉川弘文館}} * 大川原竜一, ed. {{Cite book|title=日本古代史大辞典|publisher=大和書房|isbn=4479840656}} * {{Cite book|author=宝賀寿男|title=古代氏族系譜集成 中巻|publisher=古代氏族研究会}} * 李在碩 (I, Jesoku), "Sixth-Century Yamato-Baekje Officials," ''Komazawa Historical Journal''.
== See Also == * {{ill|Hozumi clan|ja|穂積氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hozumi clan|qq=}} * {{ill|List of people in the Asuka period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of people in the Asuka period|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11665973}}
{{translated page|ja|穂積押山|version=99319506|comment=Imported full ja history}}
